summary: The hypoglossal nerve (option A), glossopharyngeal nerve (B), trigeminal nerve (C), or vagus nerve (D). Cranial nerve XI (glossopharyngeal) is most accurately identified as uninvolved since primary functions do not include processing smells; instead they serve other critical bodily processes like swallowing reflexes and salivation regulation without contributing significantly towards detecting odors through specialized receptors within our noses designed explicitly just that purpose alone
